steps.text: Find the proportion of supporters in the sample: $$\frac{6}{20} = 0.30$$
Apply this proportion to the full population of 50 council members: $$0.30 \times 50 = 15$$

• A — 6 is just the raw sample count, not scaled to the full population. Eliminated.
  • B — 9 does not correspond to any proportional calculation from these numbers. Eliminated.
  • C — 15 equals $0.30 \times 50$, the sample proportion applied to the full council. That confirms C is correct.
  • D — 30 would imply 60% support, but the sample showed only 30%. Eliminated.
